title: STANDARD FOR CANNED APPLESAUCE CXS 17-1981 Adopted in 1981. Revised in 2001. Amended in 2017, 2022.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR CANNED APPLESAUCE CXS 17-1981 Adopted in 1981. Revised in 2001. Amended in 2017, 2022.

sections.summary: Notes that following the Forty‑fifth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission (Dec 2022), amendments were made to Section 8.2 concerning labelling of non‑retail containers.

Scope: this standard applies to canned applesauce as defined in Section 2 when offered for direct consumption (including catering or repacking), but does not apply when the product is indicated as intended for further processing.

Describes canned applesauce including product definition (prepared from washed, clean apples, with/without permitted ingredients, heat‑processed and hermetically sealed) and styles (sweetened, unsweetened, and other styles) with composition and processing requirements and labelling conditions for other styles.

Defines canned applesauce as a comminuted or chopped product made from washed, clean apples (which may be peeled), prepared with or without permitted ingredients, and heat processed before or after hermetic sealing to prevent spoilage.

Defines product styles: sweetened, unsweetened, and other styles, and states requirements for soluble solids for sweetened/unsweetened types and conditions for permitting other styles including distinctiveness, conformity with the standard, and adequate labelling.

sections.section: 2.2.1 Sweetened -with sugars and/or other carbohydrate sweeteners such as honey; not less than 16.5 percent total soluble solids (16.5° Brix).
sections.summary: Sweetened style: applesauce sweetened with sugars and/or other carbohydrate sweeteners (e.g., honey) containing not less than 16.5% total soluble solids (16.5° Brix).

sections.section: 2.2.2 Unsweetened -without added sweeteners; not less than 9 percent  total soluble solids (9.0 0� Brix).
sections.summary: Unsweetened style: applesauce without added sweeteners containing not less than 9.0% total soluble solids (9.0° Brix).

Other styles: permits other presentations provided they are sufficiently distinctive from defined styles, meet all applicable requirements of the standard, and are adequately described on the label to avoid misleading the consumer.

Sets out essential composition and quality factors for applesauce including permitted ingredients, quality criteria for colour/flavour/texture, classification of 'defectives' for failure to meet quality or total solids requirements (see Section 2.2), and lot acceptance rules based on an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

Defines composition of applesauce as apples (per Section 2.1(a)) and permits salt, spices, sugars (as defined by Codex) or other carbohydrate sweeteners such as honey, and water as additional ingredients.
States the basic ingredient for the product is apples as defined in Section 2.1(a).
Lists permitted additional ingredients for applesauce: salt (sodium chloride), spices, sugars as defined by Codex and other carbohydrate sweeteners such as honey, and water.

Requires applesauce to have normal colour, flavour and odour and a characteristic texture, and that defects (e.g. seeds, peel, bruised or dark particles or similar extraneous material) should not seriously affect appearance or eating quality.

Specifies applesauce must have normal colour, flavour, odour and characteristic texture and that the number, size and prominence of defects (such as seeds, peel, carpel tissue, bruised or dark particles and similar extraneous material) should not seriously affect appearance or eating quality.
Provides that any container failing to meet one or more applicable quality requirements or the total solids requirements in Section 2.2 is to be considered a 'defective'.

States a lot meets the applicable total solids requirements (Section 2.2) and the quality criteria (Section 3.2) when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

Lists permitted categories of food additives for applesauce (acidifying agents, antioxidants, flavourings) with specific additives shown in tables and maximum levels generally 'limited by GMP', and excludes flavourings that reproduce apple flavour.
Permits malic acid (INS 296) and citric acid (INS 330) as acidifying agents for applesauce with maximum levels limited by good manufacturing practice (GMP).
Permits ascorbic acid (INS 300) and erythorbic acid (INS 315) as antioxidants for applesauce with maximum levels limited by GMP, singly or in combination.
Allows natural and synthetic flavourings for applesauce provided they do not reproduce the flavour of apples, with use limited by GMP.
Requires the product to comply with the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides.
Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Covers fill of container requirements including a minimum fill of not less than 90% of the container's water capacity (defined at 20°C), classification of underfilled containers as 'defectives', and lot acceptance based on an AQL of 6.5.

Specifies container fill requirements for applesauce: minimum fill of 90% water capacity, definition of water capacity at 20°C, classification of underfilled containers as 'defectives', and lot acceptance via a sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

Requires containers to be filled so applesauce occupies at least 90% of the container's water capacity and defines water capacity as the volume of distilled water at 20°C that the sealed container will hold when completely filled.
States that any container failing to meet the minimum fill requirement (90% of container water capacity) is to be considered a 'defective'.
Provides that a lot meets the minimum fill requirement when the number of 'defectives' (per Section 7.1.2) does not exceed the acceptance number c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

Requires labelling per the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and specifies the name must be 'Applesauce' with the close-proximity designation 'Sweetened' or optionally 'Unsweetened' as appropriate and declaration of any seasoning or flavouring; non-retail containers must be labelled in accordance with CXS 346-2021.

Labelling requirement: the product name shall be 'Applesauce' and must be accompanied by 'Sweetened' if sweetened per Section 2.2.1 or may use 'Unsweetened' if meeting Section 2.2.2; any seasoning or flavouring that characterizes the product must be declared near the name, and alternative styles permitted under Section 2.2.3 must include additional words/phrases near the name to avoid misleading consumers.

sections.section: 8.1.2 If the product has been sweetened in accordance with the provisions of Section 2.2.1, the name of the product shall be accompanied by the designation 'Sweetened', in close proximity to the name of the food.
sections.summary: 'Sweetened' must be stated in close proximity to the product name when the applesauce has been sweetened in accordance with Section 2.2.1.
